questions.question: A single species with two phenotypes, 'R' and 'S', lives in a population where reproduction is based on the genotype. The fitness of each phenotype is (R) 0.5 and (S) 1.0 in the absence of any selection pressure. Assuming random mating, find the probability of each phenotype being present in the population when it has reached equilibrium. (5 marks)

Two individuals, Alice and Bob, are deciding whether to invest in a project. Alice has a probability of 0.6 of succeeding and 0.4 of failing, while Bob has a probability of 0.7 of succeeding and 0.3 of failing. In equilibrium, each player is indifferent between their strategies. If Alice invests, she expects to gain 10, while Bob expects to gain 5. What is the probability that Alice will invest when the two expect to gain 10? (5 marks)

A single population of bacteria grows according to the logistic growth function: dN/dt = rN(1-N/K), where N is the population size and r is the growth rate. If the population is at carrying capacity K when t=0, find the time it takes for the population to reach 75% of its carrying capacity, given that the growth rate r is 0.5. (5 marks)
